title: Prototype a Complex Interaction
tagline: Prototype a drag-and-drop kanban board with state changes and edge cases.
scenario: A project management tool needs a kanban board feature. Cards can be dragged between columns (To Do, In Progress, Review, Done). Some transitions have rules: a card cannot move to "Done" without an assignee, and moving to "Review" triggers a notification.
brief: Design the complete interaction specification for this kanban board. Cover drag behaviour, drop targets, transition rules, empty states, and edge cases.
deliverables: The drag-and-drop interaction specification: grab, drag, hover target highlighting, and drop animation
Transition rules: which moves are allowed, which are blocked, and how blocked moves are communicated
Empty states: what each column looks like when empty and how to add the first card
Edge cases: what happens when dragging between columns during a sync error, or when two users drag the same card
submissionGuidance: The happy path is easy. The value is in edge cases — what happens when the drop fails? What does the user see during a network error? Design for failure.

rubric.label: Drag & Drop Design
rubric.description: Is the interaction specification detailed enough for a developer to implement?

rubric.label: Transition Rules
rubric.description: Are the business rules clearly defined with appropriate error messaging?

rubric.label: Empty States
rubric.description: Are empty states helpful and encouraging (not just blank)?

rubric.label: Edge Case Coverage
rubric.description: Are failure modes and multi-user conflicts addressed?
